title: AES, Block Ciphers, and Primitive Boundaries · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed security review reconstructs aes, block ciphers, and primitive boundaries from threat, algorithm, parameter, implementation, key-custody, failure, test-vector, operations, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for AES primitive boundary and known-answer harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply AES key-size and fixed block-size contracts without confusing key, block, message, and security strength.
Explain block-cipher keyed permutation behavior and why messages require an approved mode or higher-level construction.
Validate key and block lengths before using low-level AES APIs and convert panics or errors into bounded caller behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary
steps.instruction:
review case — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 1946 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate AES key and block contracts with known-answer and implementation-boundary evidence; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish algorithm approval, implementation selection, hardware acceleration, side-channel assumptions, and Go FIPS module status.

Inspect the Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish algorithm approval, implementation selection, hardware acceleration, side-channel assumptions, and go fips module status. For Cryptography and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary, keep Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Write the review evidence note for Crypto Go Hardware Fips Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review cryptographic engineering team handles case 1837 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate AES key and block contracts with known-answer and implementation-boundary evidence; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish algorithm approval, implementation selection, hardware acceleration, side-channel assumptions, and Go FIPS module status.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish algorithm approval, implementation selection, hardware acceleration, side-channel assumptions, and Go FIPS module status.
